tools.name: push
tools.description: Send already-committed work to the remote without making a new commit. Pushes the current branch; on a branch's first push, sets the remote branch as its upstream automatically.

tools.name: show_diagnostic
tools.description: Show git repository diagnostic info: current branch, upstream, HEAD SHA, remote URLs, and status summary.

tools.name: set_vault
tools.description: Configure the git credential helper to use a specific vault path. Returns new credential helper value.

tools.name: restore_file
tools.description: Restore a single file to its HEAD state, discarding uncommitted changes. Returns restored file path.

tools.name: reset_login
tools.description: Clear stored git credentials by unsetting the credential helper in local git config. Returns confirmation.

tools.name: set_ssh
tools.description: Change a remote URL to its SSH equivalent. Returns old and new remote URLs.

tools.name: save_work
tools.description: Stage changes, commit with a message, and optionally push to remote. Returns commit SHA and push result.

tools.name: get_vault_status
tools.description: Report current git credential helper configuration. Returns helper name and scope.

tools.name: test_login
tools.description: Test whether git credentials are working by attempting ls-remote against the origin. Returns success/failure and remote refs count.

tools.name: show_releases
tools.description: List all tags/releases in the repository, newest first. Returns tag names, target SHAs, and annotation messages.

tools.name: show_change
tools.description: Show the diff for a specific file or all changed files. Returns unified diff text.

tools.name: new_release
tools.description: Create an annotated git tag and push it to the remote. Returns the tag name and target commit SHA.

tools.name: find_changes
tools.description: List changed files: staged, unstaged, and untracked. Returns file counts and paths by status group.

tools.name: show_remote
tools.description: Show all remote names and their fetch/push URLs. Returns list of remotes with URL and type.

tools.name: get_updates
tools.description: Fetch from remote and show incoming commits not yet in local branch. Returns count and commit summaries.

tools.name: undo_changes
tools.description: Discard uncommitted changes. If filePath given, restores that file; otherwise restores all tracked files. Returns list of restored paths.

tools.name: switch_work
tools.description: Switch to an existing branch. Returns the branch switched to and current HEAD SHA.

tools.name: search_history
tools.description: Search commit history for commits whose message matches a search term. Returns matching commits with SHA, author, date, and message.

tools.name: show_history
tools.description: Show recent commit history. Returns commits with SHA, author, date, and message.

tools.name: new_work_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch from current HEAD and switch to it. Returns branch name and base commit SHA.

tools.name: set_token
tools.description: Store a personal access token for HTTP git authentication. Returns confirmation (token value is never echoed).

tools.name: clear_junk
tools.description: Remove untracked files and directories from the working tree (git clean -fd). Returns list of removed paths.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "reset_login" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Clear stored git credentials by unsetting the credential helper in local git config. Returns confirmation.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "test_login" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Test whether git credentials are working by attempting ls-remote against the origin. Returns success/failure and remote refs count.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: show_releases, show_remote, search_history · Private data: set_vault, restore_file, reset_login, set_ssh, get_vault_status, test_login, show_change, find_changes, undo_changes, set_token, clear_junk · Public sink: push · Destructive: reset_login, clear_junk.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: show_releases, show_remote, search_history · Private data: set_vault, restore_file, reset_login, set_ssh, get_vault_status, test_login, show_change, find_changes, undo_changes, set_token, clear_junk · Public sink: push · Destructive: reset_login, clear_junk

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
